text:
It is widely accepted amongst the scientific community that life originated in water. Some researchers believe that life may have originated in small water pools with changing environmental conditions rather than in the oceans. Hot springs are examples of such environments. In India, the hot springs of Puga Valley in Ladakh maintain very high temperatures (nearly at the boiling point of water) even in a cold climate. These environmental conditions seem to be similar to those on the early Earth, about 3.5 billion years ago. The organisms living in these hot springs are mostly heat-loving bacteria called thermophiles, which are

All living organisms are made up of cells. The cell represents the basic level at which life exists. Some organisms, such as bacteria or yeast, consist of only one cell (unicellular), while others like plants, fish, birds or humans are made up of millions of cells (multicellular) that work together. headings.text: Cell: The Building Block of Life

diagrams.label: Cell Structure Diagram
diagrams.description: A large, circular diagram representing a cell, positioned centrally on the page. The cell boundary is visible, containing various internal structures. Inside the cell, there is a large central vacuole, and various organelles are depicted, including mitochondria (labeled), chloroplasts (labeled), and a nucleus (labeled). Surrounding the cell, various elements are shown, including water droplets, and external structures like plant roots and leaves, suggesting the cell's environment and function. The overall color scheme uses blues, greens, and browns.
